subject: Project Management
title: The Titanic
description: A quick win: understand The Titanic.

hook.text: The Titanic was called 'unsinkable.' On April 15, 1912, it sank on its maiden voyage, killing 1,517 people.

buildup.text: The ship had too few lifeboats — only enough for 1,178 of 2,224 passengers. Regulations were outdated.

discovery.text: Iceberg warnings from other ships were received but ignored. The radio operator was busy with passenger messages.

twist.text: The Titanic's design did have watertight compartments — but they didn't extend high enough to contain the flooding.

climax.text: The Titanic disaster rewrote maritime safety law: enough lifeboats, 24-hour radio, and ice patrols became mandatory.

punchline.text: The 'unsinkable' label was the most dangerous risk of all.

quiz.question: What was a key risk factor on the Titanic?
options: Too few lifeboats and ignored iceberg warnings
Passengers caused a fire on board
